mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor market conditions, indicated by rising payrolls and declining unemployment, signal robust consumer spending which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with simultaneous PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 270 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 270 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, driving corporate revenue and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1500k and RSAFS > 600k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL, leads to higher borrowing costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL > 270 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 100 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A labor market overheating condition, indicated by UNRATE below 5% and PAYEMS growth above 100k, signals inflationary pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates a late-cycle expansion, which often precedes market corrections and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 19000 and HOUST > 1500k are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
